Phase 2 publishes the geographic data that *can support* naval (Port settlements have port metadata; sea polygons exist). Phase 3 specifies fleet types, port loading, sea zones, naval combat, weather-on-sea, Schoenland mediation, Maritime Forgetting interaction.

modules_verified.name: Phase 4 Geography Stress Tests (3 scenarios)

1_mountain_pass_t10_t11.result: Phase 3 mechanics produce coherent narrow-pass-clamp emergence; defender +3D differential
1_mountain_pass_t10_t11.spec_gaps: None (calibration playtest recommendation only — pass −1D may need reduction or Guts-pool conversion)

2_open_field_cavalry_t2.result: Phase 3 mechanics produce coherent declaratory-flanking + geometry-restricted resolution; cavalry advantage at 1.5× march budget + plains-flanking +1D

3_coastal_landing_t16_t13.result: Phase 4 stress testing reveals Phase 2 substrate gap — no canonical sea-zone adjacency between non-T1 coastal territories
3_coastal_landing_t16_t13.spec_gaps: P1: ED-055 naval scope must populate sea-zone polygon+adjacency definition before amphibious operations are canonically resolvable

modules_verified.decision: ED-781 closure — Phase 3 verified for land-based mass battle; Phase 4 surfaces P1 sea-zone adjacency gap that ED-055 must close
key_findings: Mountain pass narrow-clamp emerges from geometry (canonical correctness)
Open-field flanking is declaratory but geometry-restricted (Phase 3 contribution)
Coastal/amphibious operations require ED-055 substrate work — Phase 4 surfaces this as P1 blocker for Schoenland-pivot scenarios
